stories.headline: MacKinnon sits out Avalanche's first post-Olympic game against Utah Mammoth for maintenance
stories.summary:
Colorado Avalanche star Nathan MacKinnon will not play in the team's first game back from the 2026 Winter Olympics, sitting out Wednesday's matchup against the Utah Mammoth in Salt Lake City for what a team spokesman described as routine maintenance. It marks the first game MacKinnon has missed this season.

MacKinnon returned from Milan having played all six games for Canada at the Olympics, recording four goals and seven points. Canada fell to the United States 2-1 in overtime in the gold-medal game, earning MacKinnon and his teammates the silver medal.

The absence is notable given MacKinnon's position in the Hart Trophy and Ted Lindsay Award races. He leads the NHL with 40 goals this season and sits second in overall scoring with 93 points, trailing only Edmonton's Connor McDavid.

The Avalanche, who recently added Brett Kulak via trade, are navigating a critical stretch of the season as they manage the health of their top players following the Olympic break.

stories.headline: Nuggets post season-best defensive effort in 103-84 win over Celtics
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets held the Boston Celtics to 84 points Wednesday night at Ball Arena, the fewest points Denver has allowed all season, winning 103-84 in what coach David Adelman called a turning point in the team's defensive development. It was only the third time this season the Nuggets kept an opponent under 100 points.

Nikola Jokic led Denver with 30 points, 12 rebounds and six assists. Julian Strawther added 12 points in another start, while Bruce Brown drew praise for his ball pressure on Celtics star Jaylen Brown, picking his pocket multiple times. Denver outscored Boston 42-28 in the paint and 23-9 on second-chance points.

The win was significant given Denver's recent skid — six losses in nine games, including seven straight against teams with winning records. The Celtics entered the game having won nine of their last 10, making the margin of victory particularly notable.

Adelman credited adjustments made after Sunday's loss to Golden State, specifically improved rotations to neutralize perimeter shooters. Jokic noted the departure from Denver's offense-driven identity: 'Usually, maybe we win with the offense, but today we won with the defense.'

stories.headline: Jamal Murray cleared to play vs. Celtics despite hamstring tightness
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets upgraded All-Star guard Jamal Murray to available for Wednesday's nationally televised game against the Boston Celtics after he had been listed as probable with right hamstring tightness. Murray had been managing the issue for several games but had not missed any playing time.

Murray is averaging 25.5 points, 4.4 rebounds and 7.5 assists per game this season, shooting 48.3 percent from the field and 42.3 percent from three-point range. He earned his first career All-Star selection this season. His presence alongside Jokic was considered critical given the absences of Aaron Gordon, Peyton Watson and Jalen Pickett.

Julian Strawther was also upgraded to available after being listed as probable with a toe sprain. Strawther has started the last six games and averaged 15.5 points per game in that stretch while shooting 50.7 percent from the field.

The game carried added weight for a Nuggets team that had lost seven consecutive games against teams with winning records entering Wednesday night.

stories.headline: El Paso County jury convicts man of second-degree murder in 2024 stabbing death of UCCS professor
stories.summary:
An El Paso County jury found Ceasar Wilson, 54, guilty on all charges Thursday, including second-degree murder, in connection with the 2024 stabbing death of University of Colorado Colorado Springs Professor Haleh Abghari. The verdict was returned after deliberations concluded in Colorado Springs.

Abghari was killed in 2024 in a case that drew widespread attention at UCCS and in the broader Colorado Springs academic community. Wilson faced the full set of charges brought by prosecutors, and the jury's verdict covered all counts.

The conviction closes a case that had drawn significant attention to campus and community safety in the Colorado Springs area. Sentencing information was not immediately available following the verdict.
